title: Seeds, Origins, Paths, Query Space, Budgets, and Stop Policy · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded crawl-scope policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Record each seed URL, stakeholder, purpose, authorization source, expected content class, and crawl revision.
Define allowed schemes, normalized hosts, ports, subdomains, redirects, and cross-origin resources with deny-by-default rules.
Express permitted paths, methods, query keys, values, pagination, fragments, and content types as testable admission rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define automatic stop signals, manual kill paths, owner contact, partial-output handling, cleanup, and restart authorization.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Stop And Contact Policy
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-stop-and-contact-policy-2vexlk" that uses case 3249 to validate seed identity, origin and path admission, resource budgets, and automatic and manual stop policy. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Stop And Contact Policy.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Stop And Contact Policy into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Stop And Contact Policy, keep Stop And Contact Policy connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-stop-and-contact-policy-2vexlk
# Competency: scraper-stop-and-contact-policy.
# Case 3249: a clean package and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly.
def evidence_scraper_stop_and_contact_policy_2vexlk(admitted_origins: int = 1, admitted_paths: int = 8, page_budget: int = 240, byte_budget: int = 12000000, stop_paths: int = 5) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_2vexlk = "2vexlk-klxev2-3249"
    assert evidence_variant_2vexlk
    evidence_axes_2vexlk = (
        "authorization2vexlk",
        "url3249",
        "responseklxev2",
        "tree2vexlk3249",
        "frontier32492vexlk",
        "reportklxev23249",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_2vexlk)) == 6
    if admitted_origins != 1:
        return False, admitted_origins, "origin-scope"
    if admitted_paths < 1:
        return False, admitted_paths, "path-policy"
    if page_budget < 1 or byte_budget < 1024:
        return False, page_budget, "resource-budget"
    if stop_paths < 3:
        return False, stop_paths, "stop-policy"
    assert admitted_origins == 1 and stop_paths >= 3
    return True, page_budget, "bounded-crawl-scope"
